{{Infobox character
| name = Gilad
| appears_in = The Traveler's Gate Trilogy
| titles = Traveler
| abilities = summon fire from the sky, Naraka brand, technique that creates an almost-invisible ripple from his hand, summon a bright orange fireball made of screaming faces, Helgard summons
| introduced = tg-01-house-of-blades
}}

''See also: [[Gilad Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|Gilad is a young [[Traveler]] recognized for his exceptional abilities, often being referred to as a genius. He possesses the rare capability of maintaining bonds with two separate [[Territories]], allowing him to draw power from both [[Helgard]] and [[Naraka]] with similar proficiency. His combat prowess is significant; he once defeated a group of Itasas Tribesmen and a hunting squad sent by [[Damasca]]. Following a period of being pursued by dangerous predators in the wild, he successfully infiltrated a [[Damascan]] outpost and utilized an unguarded waypoint to open a Gate, which allowed him to return home despite landing in the middle of an enemy camp. While highly respected due to his unique connections, he can also appear timid or easily flustered in social interactions. He has demonstrated skills in detecting traps, performing summons, and using glowing red pebbles to interact with flames.}}

== House of Blades ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|Gilad is a genius [[Traveler]] hailing from the free city of [[Enosh]]. He possesses a [[Naraka brand]] on his right palm, which is marked by a red symbol that leaves streaks of light in the air as he moves. Through his unique abilities, he can summon fire from the sky and perform a technique that generates an almost-invisible ripple from his hand. As a [[Traveler]], he maintains significant bonds to two different [[Territories]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Gilad]] is a [[Traveler]] from the free city of [[Enosh]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4">''House of Blades'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Gilad]] has a red symbol on his palm that leaves streaks of light in the air.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Gilad]] can summon fire from the sky.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c4" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Gilad]] is a [[Traveler]] with bonds to two different [[Territories]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16">''House of Blades'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Gilad]] is a genius.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Gilad]]'s right palm carries his [[Naraka brand]].<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|[[Gilad]] can perform a technique that creates an almost-invisible ripple from his hand.<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|The [[Traveler]] Gilad, who wields a long-handled brand, was discovered lying unconscious on the grass outside [[Elysia]]. During his journey, he infiltrated a [[Damascan]] outpost in [[Naraka]] and successfully opened a Gate to return to his own world. While attempting a [[Helgard]] summons, he was cut off mid-chant, yet he still managed to singlehandedly overpower a squad of [[Travelers]] sent by [[Damasca]] along with the Itasas Tribesmen. Utilizing his abilities, he can summon a bright orange fireball composed of screaming faces, and he was later seen standing behind [[Alin]].}}

{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] singlehandedly overpowered the Itasas Tribesmen and a squad of [[Travelers]] sent by [[Damasca]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] infiltrated a [[Damascan]] outpost in [[Naraka]] and opened a Gate to travel back to their world.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] can summon a bright orange fireball made of screaming faces.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] can perform a [[Helgard]] summons.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] was cut off mid-chant.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] is standing behind [[Alin]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] is a character who was lying unconscious on the grass outside [[Elysia]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] is a person who uses a long-handled brand.<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c19">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|[[Gilad]] is a [[Traveler]].<ref name="tg-02-crimson-vault-c21">''The Crimson Vault'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|"They don't call me a genius for nothing, I guess."<ref name="tg-01-house-of-blades-c16" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Traveler's Gate nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:House of Blades]]
[[Category:The Crimson Vault]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate]]
[[Category:Traveler's Gate characters]]
[[Category:Travelers]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
